svg.text: AI Agent + RAG — Complete Flow Diagram
How the LLM, Tools, Vector Database, and FastAPI all connect
① AGENT FLOW — POST /chat (LLM + Google Search Tool)
Step types you see in /chat response:
② RAG FLOW — POST /documents/rag-chat (Your Knowledge Base)
How similarity search works inside ChromaDB:
Your question vector is compared to every stored document vector using cosine similarity (angle between vectors).
Score 1.0 = identical meaning | Score 0.8+ = very related | Score 0.5 = somewhat related | Score 0.0 = unrelated
"Answer using ONLY this context: [Document 1 | similarity: 0.94] RAG stands for Retrieval...
[Document 2 | similarity: 0.81] A vector database stores... QUESTION: What is RAG?"
③ Agent vs RAG — What's the difference?
• Good for: current events, general knowledge
• Answer can include anything from the web
• You do NOT control the source of truth
• You control exactly what knowledge exists
• Good for: private data, internal docs, FAQs
• LLM cannot make up info beyond the context
• YOU control the source of truth
④ Code Structure — which file does what
Entry point. Creates FastAPI app, registers all routers. Nothing else.
Calls Gemini interactions API, gets steps
Dashed border = hidden from end-users (thought steps inside the LLM)
⑤ MCP FLOW — POST /mcp-chat (LLM picks its own tools: Google Search OR ChromaDB)
Model Context Protocol — an open standard (like USB-C for AI tools). You build an MCP server that exposes functions.
Any LLM that speaks MCP can discover and call those functions autonomously. Gemini connects to your server at http://localhost:8001/mcp
Tools exposed by mcp_server/server.py (LLM discovers these automatically):
• search_knowledge_base(query, top_k) — searches ChromaDB by semantic similarity, returns matching documents
• add_document_to_kb(text, topic) — embeds text and stores it in ChromaDB • get_kb_stats() — counts documents in the KB
⑥ MULTI-AGENT FLOW — POST /multi-agent-chat (Orchestrator coordinates 3 specialist agents)
Instead of one LLM doing everything, create SPECIALIST agents — each focused on one job. Your code (orchestrator) decides which runs and in what order.
KEY difference from MCP: in MCP the LLM picks its tools autonomously; in Multi-Agent YOUR CODE controls the sequence and every agent’s output is separately visible.
Analogy: a research team — researcher + archivist + editor, each doing their job. Combined result is higher quality than one person doing everything.
Response fields: multi_agent_summary • agents[].agent_name • agents[].role • agents[].answer • agents[].flow_summary • final_answer
Multi-Agent vs MCP: Multi-Agent = YOUR CODE controls the order (predictable, transparent). MCP = LLM decides autonomously. Both use internet + KB; sources differ in who chooses.
